activation_scenario: An autonomous agent is allowed to change one artifact while being scored by an external evaluator or rubric.
behavior_change: Classify surfaces as locked, editable, append-only, or human-controlled before the loop starts; prevent the agent from using changed evaluators to approve its own work.

activation_scenario: A research or implementation loop may run past a context window, be resumed by another agent, or require auditability.
behavior_change: Create a run directory with THREAD.md, source queue, evaluations, proposals, reports, and append-only logs before executing the loop.

activation_scenario: A skill, source evaluation, manifest, or run artifact can be checked without model judgment.
behavior_change: Run deterministic checks for structure, schemas, line caps, manifest sync, retrieval status, and rubric math before invoking LLM judges.

activation_scenario: A proposed skill change may duplicate existing corpus guidance or prior run artifacts.
behavior_change: Compare mechanism summary, activation scenario, behavior change, and failure modes against the mechanism registry before drafting or publishing a skill change.

activation_scenario: Two candidate skill revisions target the same source evidence or activation scenario.
behavior_change: Run deterministic structure checks, then compare candidates with a pairwise rubric using behavior improvement, evidence fidelity, activation clarity, corpus fit, and simplicity tie-breakers.
failure_modes: verbose candidate wins by length bias

activation_scenario: An agent must explain why an external RDF world state became a belief, desire, intention, and plan.
behavior_change: Model world states, beliefs, desires, intentions, justifications, validity intervals, and plans as linked entities before reasoning or projecting results back to RDF.

activation_scenario: A long-running agent session needs compaction without losing files, decisions, risks, and next actions.
behavior_change: Maintain a structured summary and merge only newly truncated content into existing sections instead of regenerating the whole summary each time.

activation_scenario: A false claim, tool error, or hallucinated fact enters context and starts recurring in later reasoning.
behavior_change: Identify the poisoning point, truncate or restart before it, reload verified context only, and record rejected provenance rather than layering corrections over the bad context.

activation_scenario: A corpus, tool catalog, or documentation set is too large to load fully by default.
behavior_change: Load names, descriptions, or indexes first; load full artifacts only when activation conditions match the current task.

activation_scenario: Verbose tool outputs dominate the active context but may need to be retrieved later.
behavior_change: Replace resolved outputs with compact references and summaries while preserving full content in a retrievable external store.
failure_modes: stale summaries treated as ground truth

activation_scenario: Hosted agents must start background coding sessions without cold-start setup dominating user-perceived latency.
behavior_change: Pre-build environment images, keep warm sandboxes available, sync branch deltas at session start, and snapshot session state before teardown.

activation_scenario: A worker needs task-relevant orchestrator trajectory state without replaying the full trajectory as text.
behavior_change: Score trajectory KV positions using the worker task prompt, retain a shared global token mask, and tune compaction against task accuracy and token savings.

activation_scenario: An agent needs persistent memory but the required retrieval semantics are not yet proven.
behavior_change: Start with the simplest persistent layer that satisfies retrieval needs, then add vector, graph, temporal, or hybrid memory only when measured retrieval quality requires it.

activation_scenario: A task exceeds one agent's useful context or decomposes into independent subtasks.
behavior_change: Partition work across agents with isolated contexts, explicit handoffs, validation checkpoints, and measured coordination overhead.

activation_scenario: An LLM project processes many items through repeatable stages and needs resumability, debugging, and selective reruns.
behavior_change: Represent stage completion with files in per-item directories so acquire, prepare, process, parse, and render stages are idempotent and cacheable.

activation_scenario: An agent must choose and call tools from descriptions without human documentation lookup.
behavior_change: Write tool descriptions that specify purpose, activation conditions, parameters, return shape, and actionable recovery errors as an executable contract.

activation_scenario: A self-modifying loop must decide whether to merge a proposed edit to its own harness, workflow, or context mechanism.
behavior_change: Evaluate every candidate on a held-in split that checks the targeted weakness was resolved and a proposer-invisible held-out split that checks nothing else regressed; accept only when neither split regresses and at least one strictly improves, under repeated evaluation for stochastic scoring; log rejected candidates with evidence so they are not rediscovered.

activation_scenario: A meta-level search loop accumulates more candidate history than fits any context window and the proposer must learn from it.
behavior_change: Store each candidate as a directory of source, scores, and raw execution traces; let the proposer navigate the archive with file-search tools instead of receiving pre-written summaries; select parents with offspring-count discounting and reject near-duplicates by embedding similarity before paying evaluation cost.
failure_modes: diversity collapse from hill-climbing the latest candidate

activation_scenario: A loop is permitted to rewrite its own prompts, harness code, or optimizer code while budgets, permissions, and evaluators must continue to hold.
behavior_change: Enforce budgets, permissions, sandboxing, and scoring in the runtime outside every editable surface; keep evaluator source and instrumentation invisible to the proposer; programmatically re-verify locked regions after each candidate; count detected exploits as failed candidates rather than high scores.

activation_scenario: A hard problem is being handed to a long-running autonomous agent or orchestration and success cannot be checked by machine.
behavior_change: Before launch, define every load-bearing term including degenerate cases, state one exact success predicate with explicit quantifiers and scope, enumerate the non-counting near misses by name, and give auditors an enumerated domain-specific failure-mode checklist including the domain's circularity analogue.
failure_modes: circular use of an equivalent statement

activation_scenario: A long-running agent must decide when it is allowed to stop working and return a result.
behavior_change: Phrase the return condition as a predicate over the artifact (it survives adversarial audit against the enumerated checklist), pair every persistence instruction with a verification gate of matching strength, scope any partial-result fallback to external budget exhaustion only, and require artifact-based reporting with claims traceable to session evidence.

activation_scenario: An orchestrator manages many parallel workers searching an open-ended solution space.
behavior_change: Start with a genuinely diverse approach portfolio, keep early-round workers blind to the currently favored approach, maintain a registry of approach families keyed to the underlying idea rather than wording, mark routes blocked at goal-strength gaps and reopen them only for a materially new mechanism, and cross-pollinate only after routes have developed independently.
